name: Sour Cherry Syrup
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugar and water in a porcelain or enamel pan.
2. Boil the mixture gently for 15 to 20 minutes until it thickens.
3. Add the sour cherries and boil gently another 20 to 30 minutes, or until the syrup thickens.
4. Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing out all of the juice from the cherries.
5. Stir in vanilla extract.
6. Bottle the syrup.
7. To serve, add water and ice as desired.
Dried sour cherries can be used instead of fresh ones if preferred; soak them in cold water for at least 6 hours before using.
The syrup will keep at room temperature once bottled. After opening, refrigerate it.
